items.title: Sandhills Medical Says Ransomware Breach Affects 170,000
items.summary:
It took the healthcare organization nearly one year to publicly disclose a data breach after it was targeted by Inc Ransom.
The post Sandhills Medical Says Ransomware Breach Affects 170,000 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google Fixes CVSS 10 Gemini CLI CI RCE and Cursor Flaws Enable Code Execution
items.summary:
Google has addressed a maximum severity security flaw in Gemini CLI -- the "@google/gemini-cli" npm package and the "google-github-actions/run-gemini-cli" GitHub Actions workflow -- that could have allowed attackers to execute arbitrary commands on host systems.
"The vulnerability allowed an unprivileged external attacker to force their own malicious content to load as Gemini configuration,"

items.title: Silver Fox uses the new ABCDoor backdoor to target organizations in Russia and India
items.summary: The Silver Fox group is targeting companies in Russia and India by impersonating tax authorities to distribute ValleyRAT and the new ABCDoor backdoor.

items.title: SoftBank is creating a robotics company that builds data centers — and already eyeing a $100B IPO
items.summary: You need infrastructure to build AI a and robots, but apparently you also need AI and robots to build infrastructure.

items.title: The Zig project's rationale for their firm anti-AI contribution policy
items.summary:
Zig has one of the most stringent anti-LLM policies of any major open source project:

No LLMs for issues.
No LLMs for pull requests.
No LLMs for comments on the bug tracker, including translation. English is encouraged, but not required. You are welcome to post in your native language and rely on others to have their own translation tools of choice to interpret your words.

The most prominent project written in Zig may be the Bun JavaScript runtime, which was acquired by Anthropic in December 2025 and, unsurprisingly, makes heavy use of AI assistance.
Bun operates its own fork of Zig, and rec

items.title: Amazon’s cloud business is surging — and so is its capital spending
items.summary: The e-commerce giant is making more money than expected from AWS but it's also spending a lot, and will continue to do so in the near term, its chief executive said.

items.title: Sources: Anthropic could raise a new $50B round at a valuation of $900B
items.summary: The maker of Claude has received multiple pre-emptive offers at valuations in the $850 billion to $900 billion range, according to sources familiar with the matter.

items.title: Meta is still burning money on AR/VR

items.title: Satya Nadella says he’s ready to ‘exploit’ the new OpenAI deal

items.title: Microsoft says it has over 20M paid Copilot users, and they really are using it
items.summary: Despite the lingering perception that no one really uses Copilot, Microsoft said on Wednesday that the number of users and engagement is growing.

items.title: Official SAP npm packages compromised to steal credentials
items.summary: Multiple official SAP npm packages were compromised in what is believed to be a TeamPCP supply-chain attack to steal credentials and authentication tokens from developers' systems. [...]

items.title: House approves spy program on second attempt, Senate fate murky
items.summary: The bill, which passed 235-191, would renew Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act for three years.

items.title: Google Cloud surpasses $20B, but says growth was capacity-constrained
items.summary: Google Cloud topped $20B in quarterly revenue for the first time, fueled by surging demand for AI. But capacity constraints mean it could have grown even faster.

items.title: Popular WordPress redirect plugin hid dormant backdoor for years
items.summary: The Quick Page/Post Redirect plugin, installed on more than 70,000 WordPress sites, had a backdoor added five years ago that allows injecting arbitrary code into users' sites. [...]

items.title: US, China partner on scam center takedown in Dubai
